title: Blade tip extension
abstract: A tip extension for a wind turbine blade is described. The tip extension comprises an adhesive dam inside a trailing edge region of the tip extension. A chordwise position of a surface of the adhesive dam is selected such that when the tip extension is fitted to a blade, a trailing edge of the blade projects into said surface. A method of attaching a tip extension to a wind turbine blade is also described. The method comprises sliding the tip extension onto the blade such that a trailing edge of the blade projects into the adhesive dam.
claims: Claims Claims 1 . A tip extension (20) for fitting to a wind turbine blade (18), the tip extension comprising: an outer shell (28) defining a substantially hollow interior for receiving a tip end (24) of a wind turbine blade (18), the outer shell extending in a spanwise direction between an inboard end (30) and an outboard end (32), and extending in a chordwise direction between a tip extension leading edge (38) and a tip extension trailing edge (40); and an adhesive dam (57) provided inside the tip extension (20) in a trailing edge region (58), the adhesive dam extending longitudinally in the spanwise direction, wherein a chordwise position of a surface (108) of the adhesive dam (57) is selected such that when the tip extension (20) is fitted to a blade (18), a trailing edge (68) of the blade projects into said surface. 2. The tip extension (20) of Claim 1 , wherein the adhesive dam (57) is made from foam. 3. The tip extension (20) of Claim 1 or Claim 2, wherein the adhesive dam (57) is attached to an inner surface (54, 62) of the outer shell (28). 4. The tip extension (20) of any preceding claim, wherein the adhesive dam (57) is spaced apart from the tip extension trailing edge (40) in the chordwise direction to define a cavity (64) inside the tip extension between the adhesive dam and the tip extension trailing edge. 5. The tip extension (20) of Claim 4, wherein the outer shell (28) comprises first and second half shells (46, 48) bonded together by adhesive (65) provided within the cavity (64). 6. The tip extension (20) of any of Claims 1 to 3, wherein the adhesive dam (57) substantially fills the trailing edge region (58) of the tip extension. 7. The tip extension (20) of any preceding claim, wherein the inboard end (30) is open and/or the outboard end (32) is closed and/or the outboard end defines a tip of the tip extension. 8. A wind turbine blade assembly comprising a wind turbine blade (18) and a blade tip extension (20) as defined in any of Claims 1 to 7 fitted over a tip end of the blade. 9. The wind turbine blade assembly of Claim 8, wherein the trailing edge of the blade (68) inside the tip extension (20) is sandwiched between parts of the adhesive dam (57). 10. A method of attaching a tip extension (20) to a wind turbine blade (18), the method comprising: providing a tip extension (20) as defined in any of Claims 1 to 7; and sliding the tip extension onto a tip end of the blade (18) such that a trailing edge (68) of the blade projects into the adhesive dam (57). 1 1 . The method of Claim 10, further comprising providing one or more further adhesive dams (70) between an outer surface (72) of the blade (18) and an inner surface (54, 62) of the tip extension (20), wherein the one or more further adhesive dams at least partially define one or more bond cavities between the tip extension and the blade, and wherein the method further comprises supplying adhesive to the one or more bond cavities to bond the tip extension to the blade. 12. A method of making a tip extension (20) for a wind turbine blade (18), the method comprising: providing first and second half shells (46, 48) to be joined together to define an outer shell (28) of the tip extension, each half shell extending in a spanwise direction between an inboard end (30) and an outboard end (32) and extending in a chordwise direction between a tip extension leading edge (38) and a tip extension trailing edge (40); attaching an adhesive dam (57) to an inner surface (54) of the first half shell (46), the adhesive dam extending longitudinally in the spanwise direction and being spaced apart from the tip extension trailing edge (40) of the first half shell in the chordwise direction; arranging the half shells (46, 48) one on top of another to form an outer shell (28) of the tip extension defining a substantially hollow interior and such that a cavity (64) is formed inside the outer shell between the adhesive dam (57) and the aligned trailing edges (40) of the half shells; and providing adhesive (65) to the cavity (64) to bond the half shells together at their respective trailing edges. 13. The method of Claim 12, further comprising providing one or more holes (91 ) in the first and or second half shells (46, 48), said one or more holes communicating with the cavity (64) when the half shells are arranged one on top of another, and providing the adhesive (65) to the cavity via the one or more holes. 14. The method of Claim 12 or Claim 13, further comprising attaching the adhesive dam (57) to one of the half shells (46, 48) prior to bonding the half shells together. 15. The method of any of Claims 12 to 14, wherein the adhesive dam (57) is made of foam.
